entities.name: FINGER MILLET
entities.description: Eleusine coracana, a crop studied for the relationship between phytolith formation and transpiration rate

entities.name: PEARL MILLET
entities.description: Cenchrus americanus (Pennisetum glaucum), a crop studied for the relationship between phytolith formation and transpiration rate

entities.name: SORGHUM
entities.description: Sorghum bicolor, a crop studied for the relationship between phytolith formation and transpiration rate

entities.name: PHYTOLITH FORMATION
entities.description: The process of silica deposition in plant tissues, used as a proxy for environmental conditions

entities.name: TRANSPIRATION RATE
entities.description: The rate at which water is lost from the plant, investigated in relation to phytolith formation

entities.name: WATER AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The amount of water available to plants, which influences phytolith morphotypes

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: A state of limited water availability that prompts plants to alter phytolith production

entities.name: DRYLANDS
entities.description: Arid or semi-arid regions where archaeological reconstructions of water management are conducted

entities.name: LOGISTIC REGRESSION PREDICTIVE MODEL
entities.description: A statistical model built on phytolith assemblages to predict water availability

entities.name: RATIO OF SENSITIVE TO FIXED MORPHOTYPES
entities.description: A specific phytolith proxy measurement used as an indicator of water availability

entities.name: C4 CROPS
entities.description: A group of plants using the C4 photosynthetic pathway, including the studied millets and sorghum
